title: SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) meta-schema
description: Schema for Markdown SOPs stored in resources/sops/. An SOP is a methodology document: WHAT to check/do, in WHICH ORDER, with WHICH SEVERITY, and WHICH mini-skill executes each step. It does NOT contain execution logic — that lives in the mini-skills. An orchestrator skill reads an SOP and executes its sequence.
note: SOPs are stored as Markdown with YAML frontmatter. This JSON schema validates the frontmatter block. The body is free Markdown.

name.description: Slug identifying the SOP. Conventionally prefixed `audit-`, `setup-`, `roadmap-`, `hygiene-`, `diagnostic-`, etc.

category.description: High-level functional category. Used by orchestrators to discover relevant SOPs.

scope.description: Level at which the SOP operates. single_brand = one brand at a time. portfolio = multiple brands via fan-out.

platforms.description: Platforms the SOP touches. Agnostic = no platform dependency.

language.description: Primary language of the SOP content. SOPs may mix EN/FR for technical terms.

description.description: One to three sentence description of what the SOP does, for whom, and when it's used. Enough for an orchestrator to decide if this SOP is relevant.

source.description: Identifier: skill name, operator phrase, parent SOP name, schedule expression, event type.

invoked_by.description: Who or what triggers this SOP. Explicit triggers so orchestrators can match operator intent to SOP. Operator-direct means the SOP is invokable conversationally too.

requires.description: Pre-conditions that must be satisfied before the SOP can run (access tokens, data populated, schema fields present, minimum history). Fail-fast checkpoints.

mini-skill.description: Name of a mini-skill that validates a precondition before the SOP proceeds.

precondition_checks.description: Mini-skills the orchestrator runs FIRST before iterating the SOP body. If any fails, abort with operator feedback.

used_in_scenarios.description: Concrete operator contexts where this SOP is appropriate. Helps routing + orchestrator scenario selection. Free-text, human-readable.

sections.description: List of sections the output must contain. Orchestrator uses this to structure the report.

output_destination.description: File paths where output should be written. Supports `{slug}` placeholder.

estimated_tokens.description: Approximate LLM token cost. For cost planning.

replaced_by.description: Name of successor SOP if deprecated.

examples.name: audit-meta-global
examples.description: End-to-end audit methodology for a Meta Ads account on a single e-commerce brand. 40+ checkpoints organized in 7 layers.
